questions.question: In order to avoid trees at the approach end of the runway, you will fly a 4° descent angle. If your approach speed is 72 knots, what rate of descent will you target?
You are flying an approach to runway 16 at 103 KTAS, and the winds are 200° at 16 kts. What descent rate will keep you on the 4.8° descent angle?

The world's fastest recorded bird, a peregrine falcon named Frightful, was recorded traveling 242 MPH in a dive. If you were descending next to Frightful on the record-setting dive, what would your vertical speed indicator read?

You are going to be flying a steeper than normal approach path of 3.5° and the ILS glideslope is out of service so you need to figure out what FPM to fly on the approach. If your ground speed is 135 kts and you have a headwind of 20 kts on the approach, what would your FPM need to be?
An instrument approach requires a 3.2° glide slope. Your approach speed is 142 knots and there is a 5-kt tailwind. What vertical speed must you maintain on the approach?
Due to terrain, the PAPI on runway 14 has a 3.6° slope. You approach at 101 MPH TAS, and the wind is 180° at 8 KTS, what is your descent rate?
The current winds are 320° at 84 knots. Your vertical speed indicator reads a 3,300 FPM descent. Your true airspeed is 330 knots with a true course of 117°. What is your angle of descent?
What vertical speed will bring the aircraft from NIFAA to the runway threshold at the touchdown zone elevation?
questions.context: Use the following information for the remaining 5 questions. The AWOS broadcasts winds 150° at 24 KTS with a temperature of +9°C, Altimeter 29.79" Hg. The fix "NIFAA" is 11.2 nm from the threshold of runway 18. Runway 18 has a magnetic course of 177° with a variation of 5° E and is located at 1,241' MSL. The weather is the same at all altitudes.

questions.question: Flying the LOC/DME-E instrument approach into Aspen requires a descent gradient of 6.59°. What descent rate is required if you are flying 113 KIAS with a 10 knot tailwind? Assume pressure altitude of 7837' at ISA.

You are on a 1.5 NM approach to land on runway 16L. You are traveling at 90 KTAS and the current winds at the field are 170° at 10 knots. You are currently at 7,258 feet and the threshold is at 6,576 feet. What is your rate of descent?
What descent rate would be required if you wanted to maintain a 3° descent?
questions.context: Referenced question 13: The winds are reported from 240° at 17 knots while you are on final for runway 22. If you are 14 KM away how long will it take you to reach the runway if you are flying 90 KTAS?

questions.question: The descent angle on your instrument approach into Aspen is a hair-raising 6.6°. You are approaching at 112 KIAS on a true course of 151°. You have a direct headwind of 23 knots and begin your descent at 11,700' with an OAT of -2°C. If the altimeter setting is 30.41", what descent rate will you use? Assume constant TAS in descent.
What is your angle of descent if you are descending at 1800'/minute at a TAS of 285 KTS? The wind is 240° at 76 KTS, and your true course is 043°.

You are flying an approach over some buildings that requires a steeper than normal glide path of 3.5°. The tower reports a 15-kt headwind and you note your ground speed of 145 KTS. What is the required rate of descent to maintain the glide path?

You are flying an approach over some buildings that requires a steeper than normal glide path of 3.5°. As you try to calculate the required descent rate you reference your ground speed of 145 kts. The tower reported wind is a 15 kt headwind, what is the required rate of descent to maintain the glide path?

You are instructed to cross NIFAA at 12,000', and you are currently cruising at M0.72 at FL320 in a standard atmosphere with calm winds. You plan your standard descent profile of 2,500 FPM, but just as you reach your top of descent, ATC assigns 280 knots indicated. What vertical speed is required now? Assume the acceleration is instantaneous, and speed is constant in the descent.

You are following a standard 3° ILS glide slope for runway 17 (0' MSL). As you pass 3000', you know that the winds aloft are 140@16 kts, and your approach speed is 110 knots indicated. The OAT is showing 18°C, and the altimeter setting is 29.66". What descent rate should keep you on the glide slope?
What is an aircraft's descent rate in feet per second if it descends at 675 ft/sm at a ground speed of 135 knots?
You are descending on a 3° slope at 180 kts when ATC tells you to reduce to 150 kts. If you want to maintain the same 3° descent what rate of descent will you need to maintain?
An experienced skydiver plans to jump over a sporting event and land centerfield. The jump from 9000' AGL to touchdown is estimated to take 52 seconds. What is the average descent rate in knots?

You just got a job flying a skydive airplane. On one of your first runs, a jumper decides to sit this one out and ride back down with you. You remember from training that his reserve canopy will automatically deploy if it senses a descent rate over 78 MPH at 750' AGL. What is the maximum vertical speed at which you can descend without deploying his reserve?

You are cruising at FL390 and a ground speed of 265 KTS. ATC instructs you to cross VEEKS at 25,000. If you are flying a true course of 113°, and the average winds in the descent are 035°@85 KTS, how far from VEEKS should you start your descent? Your SOP prescribes a 1500 FPM descent profile.
You are flying an ILS at 90 ktas into a 15 knot headwind. What descent rate do you need to maintain the 3° glideslope?
What is your vertical speed if you are following a glideslope that results in descending 300 feet per nautical mile, with a ground speed of 128 knots?
You are descending at 100 KTAS with 22 KTS of headwind. How many FPM do you need to descend at in order to maintain a 3° descent angle?

You plan to use a nice and shallow 2.5° descent all the way to your destination. At an altitude of 35,000' with a landing elevation of 13', how far out do you need to start your descent with an average groundspeed of 358 KTS?

You were at 12,500' AGL when they jumped. After the last jumper left you immediately start a steep descent back toward the field and 156 SECS later you land. The airports elevation is 887' MSL. What was your average descent rate?
questions.context: Referenced question 30: You are flying 6 skydivers each weighing 185 LBS in a Cessna 208. Your total weight and CG before they jump is 6085 LBS and 192.9". Half of them were seated at station 230" and the other half at station 270". What is your new CG after they jump?

questions.question: At what point would he begin his descent into KTYS?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 11-15: Joey plans a flight from KMRN (1270') to KTYS (979') at 8,500' MSL on a true course of 277°. The total distance is 118 NM and he will cruise at 145 MPH TAS and 8.7 GPH with winds aloft 240° at 14 KTS. His average climb performance will be 650 FPM at 92 KTAS and 9.8 GPH with average winds of 215° at 15 KTS. His average descent performance will be 900 FPM at 110 KTAS and 5.2 GPH with average winds of 320° at 8 KTS.

questions.question: During an emergency descent your reach 2300 FPM. How fast are you descending vertically?
While you are tracking a 3.0° glideslope you are descending at 862fpm. If you are at 1,590' AGL when you are 5 NM from the runway, what is your groundspeed?
On your approach back to the airport you want a nice, stable descent (3.0°). What should your altimeter read at 8 NM prior to the runway if field elevation is 1,625' MSL?
If an aircraft flying at Mach 0.62 plans to descend at a constant 3.5° descent angle, what descent rate should be maintained if the outside air temperature is -38°C?

As you are on a 10 mile final, you have your aim point set in your windscreen right at the start of the runway but when you look down at your instruments you notice you are descending at 700 fpm and 74 TAS. You know the wind is 100@9 and you are landing runway 15L. What is your descent angle?

Your instructor has taught you that you need to be stabilized by 500'. What descent rate must you maintain to get stabilized at a 3.0° descent angle? (Assume the wind is constant through the descent).
questions.context: Referenced question 1: As you are on a 10 mile final, you have your aim point set in your windscreen right at the start of the runway but when you look down at your instruments you notice you are descending at 700 fpm and 74 TAS. You know the wind is 100@9 and you are landing runway 15L. What is your descent angle?

questions.question: If you are descending at 860 fpm at a groundspeed of 144 knots, what is your descent in feet/NM?
An airplane descends from 9500 feet to 2800 feet at a rate of 830 fpm. It traveled 34.7 sm during the descent. What was its ground speed?
